title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Ignore Embedded Fields

statement.markdown:
Given a MongoDB document, write a function that projects a specific field while ignoring any embedded fields. For example, if the input document is

```json
{ 'name': 'John', 'address': { 'street': '123 Main St', 'city': 'Anytown', 'state': 'CA' } }
```and the desired field is 'name', the function should return

```json
{ 'name': 'John' }
```

However, if the desired field is 'address', the function should return the entire 'address' object, including its embedded fields.
statement.inputFormat: MongoDB document as a JSON string
statement.outputFormat: Projected document as a JSON string
statement.notes: The function should handle nested fields and ignore any fields that are not explicitly mentioned in the projection.

hints.text: Use the MongoDB $project operator to specify the fields to include in the projection

editorial.title: Editorial: Fix MongoDB Projection to Ignore Embedded Fields
editorial.intuition: The function should recursively traverse the document and only include the fields that are explicitly mentioned in the projection
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to use a recursive function to traverse the document and build the projected document
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use the MongoDB $project operator to specify the fields to include in the projection
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses the $project operator and handles nested fields by recursively projecting the sub-documents
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function correctly projects the specified fields and ignores any embedded fields
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the optimal solution
commonMistakes: Including embedded fields in the projection

functionSignature.python: def projectField(self, document: dict, field: str) -> dict:
